```{r}
library(sf)
library(dplyr)
library(data.table)
library(tigris)
library(lubridate)
options(tigris_use_cache = TRUE)

## Create an empirical storm effects model

library(sf)
library(dplyr)

storm_intensity <- 34 # all tropical storms and hurricanes
begin_year <- 2004
end_year <- 2022

L <- "https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/data/international-best-track-archive-for-climate-stewardship-ibtracs/v04r00/access/shapefile/IBTrACS.NA.list.v04r00.lines.zip"
  
if(!"IBTrACS.NA.list.v04r00.lines.zip" %in% list.files(here::here("data"))) {
download.file(url = L,
              destfile = here::here("data",
                                    "IBTrACS.NA.list.v04r00.lines.zip"))
unzip(here::here("data", "IBTrACS.NA.list.v04r00.lines.zip"),
      exdir = here::here("data"))
}

Tracks.sf <- st_read(dsn = here::here("data"), 
                     layer = "IBTrACS.NA.list.v04r00.lines") |>
  st_transform(crs = 32616)

Tracks.sf <- Tracks.sf |>
  filter(year >= begin_year & year <= end_year) |>
  filter(USA_WIND >= storm_intensity) |>
  select(SID, SEASON, year, month, day, hour, min,
         NAME, SUBBASIN, ISO_TIME, USA_WIND, USA_PRES, USA_RMW, USA_EYE, USA_ROCI)

Tracks.sf |>
  st_drop_geometry() |>
  summarize(avgRMW = mean(USA_RMW, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.852,
                   avgEYE = mean(USA_EYE, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.852,
                   avgROCI = mean(USA_ROCI, na.rm = TRUE) * 1.852)

# Fill in missing RMW values. Start with pedigree, then use minimum pressure, and finish again with pedigree
Tracks.sf <- Tracks.sf |>
  group_by(SID) |>  # pedigree
  mutate(USA_RMW = ifelse(is.na(USA_RMW), mean(USA_RMW, na.rm = TRUE), USA_RMW))

Tracks.sf <- Tracks.sf |>
  group_by(USA_PRES) |> # minimum pressure
  mutate(USA_RMW = ifelse(is.na(USA_RMW), mean(USA_RMW, na.rm = TRUE), USA_RMW))

Tracks.sf <- Tracks.sf |>
  group_by(SID) |> # pedigree
  mutate(USA_RMW = ifelse(is.na(USA_RMW), mean(USA_RMW, na.rm = TRUE), USA_RMW))

#Add a buffer to the tracks to make segmented wind swaths
Swaths.sf <- Tracks.sf |>
  st_buffer(dist = Tracks.sf$USA_RMW * 1852) # 1852 converts to meters

Boundaries.sf <- USAboundaries::us_states(resolution = "low", states = "FL") |> 
  st_transform(crs = 32616)

X <- Swaths.sf |>
  st_intersects(Boundaries.sf, sparse = FALSE) #Does the swath intersect the state border?
Swaths.sf <- Swaths.sf[X, ]
Swaths.sf <- Swaths.sf |>
  mutate(Date = lubridate::as_date(ISO_TIME)) #Add a date column

# Extract the boundaries of storm impacts by storm ID. Dissolve the overlap borders of the individual swaths by storm to create a single storm swath. Add the storm category (Saffir-Simpson) based on wind speed
Swaths.sf <- Swaths.sf |>
  group_by(SID) |>
  summarize(Date0 = first(Date),
            NAME = first(NAME),
            Wind = max(USA_WIND),
            geometry = st_union(geometry)) |>
  mutate(Storm_Category = case_when(
    Wind >= 34 & Wind <= 63 ~ 0,
    Wind >= 64 & Wind <= 82 ~ 1,
    Wind >= 83 & Wind <= 95 ~ 2,
    Wind >= 96 & Wind <= 112 ~ 3,
    Wind >= 113 & Wind <= 136 ~ 4,
    Wind >= 137 ~ 5
  ))

# Transform the geometry to a geographic CRS (4326) and unionize the swaths
sf_use_s2(TRUE)

Swaths.sf <- Swaths.sf |>
    st_transform(crs = 4326)
union_Swaths2.sfg <- Swaths.sf |>
  st_union()

# Add a stable ID for each swath row
Swaths.sf <- Swaths.sf %>% mutate(swath_id = dplyr::row_number())

# --- ZCTAs for Florida (2020), as in your code ---
zcta_us <- zctas(cb = TRUE, year = 2020) %>%
  select(zip = ZCTA5CE20, geometry)

fl <- states(cb = TRUE, year = 2020) %>%
  filter(STUSPS == "FL") %>%
  st_transform(st_crs(zcta_us))

zctas_fl <- st_join(zcta_us, fl, join = st_intersects, left = FALSE) %>%
  select(zip, geometry)

# Align CRS with Swaths.sf
zctas_fl <- st_transform(zctas_fl, st_crs(Swaths.sf))

# --- Precompute ZIP ↔ swath intersections once ---
ints <- st_intersects(zctas_fl, Swaths.sf)  # list: for each ZIP, which swath rows intersect

# Build a long mapping table of (zip, swath_id)
zcta_swath_map <- data.table(
  zip      = rep(zctas_fl$zip, lengths(ints)),
  swath_id = as.integer(unlist(ints))
)

# Keep only ZIPs that intersect at least one swath
zcta_swath_map <- unique(zcta_swath_map)
stopifnot(nrow(zcta_swath_map) > 0)

# Precompute full date grid bounds (same as your code used)
all_dates <- as.IDate(seq(as.Date("2004-12-31"), as.Date("2022-12-31"), by = "day"))

# The set of ZIPs we care about = those that intersect at least one swath
all_zips <- sort(unique(zcta_swath_map$zip))

# Full grid (ZIP × date); we will left-join effects into this
grid <- CJ(zip = as.character(all_zips), date = all_dates)
```

## Load & prep ER data

```{r}
ER_visits.dt <- data.table::fread(here::here("data", "Combined_ER_Data.csv"))
ER_visits.df <- ER_visits.dt |>
  as.data.frame()

setDT(ER_visits.df)

# Pad ZipCode to 5 chars; drop bad codes
ER_visits.df[
  , ZipCode := fifelse(ZipCode > 0 & ZipCode < 1e6, sprintf("%05d", ZipCode), NA_character_)
]
ER_visits.df[, Date := as.IDate(Date)]
ER_visits_clean <- ER_visits.df[!is.na(ZipCode) & nchar(ZipCode) == 5]

# Standardize names; optionally collapse duplicates
setnames(ER_visits_clean, c("ZipCode","Date","Total_Visits"),
                           c("zip",    "date","er_visits"))
ER_visits_clean <- ER_visits_clean[, .(er_visits = sum(er_visits, na.rm = TRUE)),
                                   by = .(zip, date)]
```

### Functions for randomization and one-run pipeline

```{r}
library(fixest)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(data.table)

# --- Parameters ---
deltaT <- 1L    # one-day increment
n_new  <- 8L    # days before & after "impact"
min_diff_days <- 16L  # minimum abs difference from original date
models_to_run <- c("logit", "poisson")
base_seed <- 123  # reproducible randomness
n_iter <- 200L    # how many randomized runs

# Randomize to a hurricane-season date (May 1–Nov 30 of the same year) >= min_diff_days from date0
sample_hurr_date <- function(date0, min_diff = 16L) {
  if (is.na(date0)) return(as.Date(NA))
  date0 <- as.Date(date0)

  yr <- year(date0)
  season_start <- as.Date(sprintf("%d-05-01", yr))
  season_end   <- as.Date(sprintf("%d-11-30", yr))
  candidates <- seq(season_start, season_end, by = "day")

  if (date0 >= season_start && date0 <= season_end) {
    diffs <- abs(as.integer(candidates - date0))
    candidates <- candidates[diffs >= min_diff]
  }
  if (!length(candidates)) candidates <- seq(season_start, season_end, by = "day")

  sample(candidates, size = 1)
}

# Helper to safely retrieve the 3 coefficients (returns NA if a term isn't estimated)
get_effect_coefs <- function(mod, want = c("effect::Cleanup", "effect::Threat", "effect::Impact")) {
  b <- coef(mod)
  out <- setNames(rep(NA_real_, length(want)), want)
  nm <- intersect(names(b), want)
  out[nm] <- b[nm]
  data.table(coef = names(out), estimate = as.numeric(out))
}

run_one_randomization <- function(iter) {
  set.seed(base_seed + iter)

  # Randomize Date0 per swath (vectorized)
  Date0_random <- vapply(Swaths.sf$Date0, sample_hurr_date, as.Date(NA), min_diff = min_diff_days)

  # Expand per swath: dates from -n_new : +n_new around Date0_random
  reps <- rep(seq_len(nrow(Swaths.sf)), each = 2L * n_new + 1L)
  dd <- data.table(
    swath_id = Swaths.sf$swath_id[reps],
    date     = as.IDate(rep(Date0_random, each = 2L * n_new + 1L) + rep((-n_new):n_new, times = nrow(Swaths.sf)))
  )

  # Assign effect labels in order: Threat (before), Impact (at 0), Cleanup (after)
  dd[, effect := rep(c(rep("Threat", n_new), "Impact", rep("Cleanup", n_new)), times = nrow(Swaths.sf))]

  # Join ZIPs via precomputed (zip, swath_id) map — no spatial ops in the loop
  zde <- merge(zcta_swath_map, dd, by = "swath_id", allow.cartesian = TRUE)[, .(zip, date, effect)]

  # Keep the single most-severe effect per ZIP–date
  sev <- data.table(effect = c("None", "Cleanup", "Threat", "Impact"), rank = 0:3)
  zde <- sev[zde, on = "effect"]
  zde <- zde[, .SD[which.max(rank)], by = .(zip, date)][, .(zip, date, effect)]

  # Build full calendar over (ZIP × all_dates), fill NA → "None"
  effect_calendar <- zde[grid, on = .(zip, date)]
  effect_calendar[is.na(effect), effect := "None"]
  effect_calendar[, effect := factor(effect, levels = c("None","Cleanup","Threat","Impact"))]

  # Merge with ER, add controls
  merged <- merge(effect_calendar, ER_visits_clean, by = c("zip", "date"), all.x = TRUE)
  merged[is.na(er_visits), er_visits := 0L]
  merged[, dow := lubridate::wday(date, label = TRUE, abbr = TRUE)]
  merged[, year_month := as.IDate(floor_date(date, unit = "month"))]

  # Hurricane season only
  dat <- as.data.frame(merged) %>%
    mutate(is_le6 = as.integer(er_visits == 6L)) %>%
    filter(lubridate::month(date) %in% 5:11)

  out_list <- list()

  if ("logit" %in% models_to_run) {
    mA <- feglm(
      is_le6 ~ i(effect, ref = "None") | zip + year_month + dow,
      data   = dat,
      family = binomial("logit"),
      cluster = ~ zip + year_month
    )
    outA <- get_effect_coefs(mA)[, `:=`(iter = iter, model = "logit")]
    out_list[["A"]] <- outA
  }

  if ("poisson" %in% models_to_run) {
    d_gt6 <- dplyr::filter(dat, er_visits > 6L)
    mB <- fepois(
      er_visits ~ i(effect, ref = "None") | zip + year_month + dow,
      data = d_gt6, 
      cluster = ~ zip + year_month
    )
    outB <- get_effect_coefs(mB)[, `:=`(iter = iter, model = "poisson")]
    out_list[["B"]] <- outB
  }

  rbindlist(out_list, use.names = TRUE)
}
```

### Run 200 randomizations and save the coefficients

```{r}
# Run with a simple progress bar
pb <- txtProgressBar(min = 0, max = n_iter, style = 3)
coef_runs <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(n_iter), function(it) {
  res <- run_one_randomization(it)
  setTxtProgressBar(pb, it)
  res
}), use.names = TRUE)
close(pb)

# Reorder columns nicely
setcolorder(coef_runs, c("iter", "model", "coef", "estimate"))

head(coef_runs)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)

coef_summary <- coef_runs %>%
  group_by(model, coef) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_est = mean(estimate, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_est   = sd(estimate, na.rm = TRUE),
    n        = sum(!is.na(estimate)),
    se_est   = sd_est / sqrt(n),
    ci_lower = mean_est - 1.96 * se_est,
    ci_upper = mean_est + 1.96 * se_est,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

coef_summary
```
